title: PACT Fabric Manifest
description: Response body for GET /api/pact/{fabricId}/manifest. Caller-scoped view of a fabric: the caller's own role, constraints, obligations, and the counterparties visible to it. Subject to §17.13 'Manifest visibility' rules — never a privacy bypass. Added in PACT v2.0.3 (§4.4.2).

spec_version.description: PACT spec version this response conforms to.

caller.description: Who the caller is, as the server resolved them.

allowed_sections.description: Section IDs the caller can access (only set when context_mode is 'section-scoped').

constraints_on_caller.description: Constraints either published by the caller or that the caller is bound by.

published_by_self.description: True if the caller published this constraint. False if another member published it and the caller is subject to it (e.g. as an information-barrier hard constraint).

pending_obligations.description: Only the caller's own pending obligations. Counterparty obligation counts appear under counterparties[].pending_obligation_count.

counterparties.description: Other members visible to the caller. PII fields (agent_name, principal_id, etc.) are elided where the caller is not entitled to see them under §17.13. Elision is by key omission, not null.

last_seen.description: Coarse-grained liveness timestamp. RECOMMENDED second-precision or coarser (§17.13).

disclosure_level.description: The §10.3 graduated-disclosure level under which this counterparty is exposed to the caller. 'constraint' = caller sees only the existence and constraints; higher levels expose more.

shared_sections.description: Sections the caller and this counterparty are both interested in (overlap of allowed_sections / non-zero salience), where the implementation chooses to surface this.

unread_event_id_from.description: Lowest event id the caller has not yet acknowledged via /mark-read (§4.4.4). Null if everything is acknowledged.

unread_event_id_to.description: Highest event id the caller has not yet acknowledged.

unread_count.description: Count of unacknowledged events. Implementations MAY return null if computing this is expensive.

snapshot_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the manifest was constructed.
